briefdescription.para: Enumeration for ASU update source types.
This function sends request to PLM to perform In-Place ASU firmware update.

parameterdescription.para: Source of the update PDI (DDR or Image Store).

listitem.para: XPLMI_ERR_INPLACE_UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS, if update is already in progress.
XPLMI_ERR_INVALID_RSVD_DDR_REGION_UPDATE, if DDR reserved region is invalid.
XLOADER_ERR_ASU_FW_NOT_FOUND, if ASU firmware not found in update PDI.
XPLMI_ERR_ASU_COMPATIBILITY_CHECK, if version compatibility check fails.
XPLMI_ERR_ASU_FW_NOT_RUNNING, if ASU firmware is not running.

briefdescription.para: This main function to call the In-Place ASU Update example function to perform ASU firmware update.

detaileddescription.para: Main function to call the user module example functions.
Main function to call the Load DDR Copy Image example function.
Main function to call the config secure communication function.
Main function to call the ExtractMetaheader example function.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if example is run successfully.
Handoff to the ROM on success.

inbodydescription.para: Perform In-Place ASU Update based on configured source.
Invoke Generate for IV, SharedMem contains random data from this call. Only 12 bytes will be used for each IV

To generate glitch using test mode, configure glitch detectors with default values. To configure glitch detector to detect glitch of at least 5% depth, 1ns width with 0.7V reference voltage, provide Depth = 0x0E, Width = 0x01, RefVoltage = 0x04.To select the register values instead of efuse values, provide UserRegVal = 0x01

briefdescription.para: This function provides In Place PLM Update support.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XPlmi_ClientInstance instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: XPLMI_ERR_UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS if a task cannot be executed as the update is in progress.
XPLMI_ERR_PLM_UPDATE_DISABLED if PLM Update is disabled in ROM_RSV efuse.
XPLMI_ERR_PLM_UPDATE_SHUTDOWN_INIT if failed to shutdown initiate of modules during InPlace PLM Update.
XPLMI_ERR_UPDATE_TASK_NOT_FOUND if PLM Update task is not found.
XPLMI_ERR_INPLACE_UPDATE_FROM_IMAGE_STORE error during update from IS.

briefdescription.para: This function provides GET BOARD command execution for user data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XPlmi_ClientInstance instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function will initialize IPI interface for Get device id.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XPlmi_ClientInstance instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This is interrupt service routine of glitch detector.

detaileddescription.para: User code can be written here. Some default actions like reading the glitch detector status and clearing IRQ status, disabling interrupt on entry and enabling at exit is done here.

briefdescription.para: This function generates glitch on given glitch detector.
This function will trigger the interrupt and send messages.
This function will trigger the interrupt and exchange messages.
When IPI channel Sends Response message from PLM which initialized in user module this function will be called and changes the status of RecvDone to 1 .

parameterdescription.para: is the reference pointer to callback function.

briefdescription.para: This Function will be called if any error occur during IPI transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is the reference pointer to callback function.
is the Error occurred during IPI transfer.

briefdescription.para: Select update source: IMG_STORE_SRC or DDR_SRC.
Image Store PDI ID containing ASU firmware.
DDR address where update PDI is loaded.
Length of debug log buffer in words.
Value to clear IRQ status of glitch detector.
Value to generate test glitch on glitch detector0.
Value to generate test glitch on glitch detector1.
Value to stop test glitch generation on both the glitch detectors.

memberdef.name: DEVICE_INT_ID
memberdef.initializer: 191U /* when using for PL,change interrupt ID here */

briefdescription.para: Response for trigger BBRAM erase valid tamper responses are 0x8 BBRAM erase 0x4 Secure lockdown 1 0x2 Secure lockdown 0 0x1 System reset 0x0 Reserved.
